mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in nominal retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms; higher 3-month RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 357013.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day SPX return spreads versus lower-growth months, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above a moderate tightening threshold raises discount rates and financing costs, reducing equity valuation multiples; FEDFUNDS increases above the threshold predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.0% and is higher than 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 3.26 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with falling unemployment identify broad labor-market expansion that supports income, consumption, and earnings; the joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 133786.0 and UNRATE latest snapshot value 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either single-series condition over 60 trading days, or if triggered 60-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in industrial production momentum signals weakening goods-sector output and earnings pressure in cyclical industries; INDPRO growth crossing below zero predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 119.657 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO slowdowns have mean 20-day SPX returns equal to or above unconditional mean returns, or if downside hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts reduces construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit-sensitive household confidence; HOUST weakness after a prior expansion predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 2004.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while policy rates fail to rise signals margin pressure and future tightening risk; the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 194.1 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 3.26
regime_hint: inflation scare with policy behind the curve
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no predictive power over CPIAUCSL alone, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth crossing into a solid expansion range confirms aggregate demand strength and raises expected corporate cash flows; higher GDPC1 growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value 11092.0 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered GDPC1 reaccelerations do not deliver positive average 60-day SPX returns or if their return distribution is not better than unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk before earnings estimates adjust; UNRATE increases beyond the threshold predict negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 5.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if average 5-day returns are not below unconditional returns.
